---
title: "Introduction"
---

Here you will find the basic instructions to start a simulation with cdmetapopR.

First, install cdmetapopR and its dependencies by following the instructions in the [README]Github page or CRAN??? 

``` R
install.packages("cdmetapopR")

``` 
CDMetaPOP can simulate very complex systems but you can get started with a very simple template. This platform and tutorial are meant to help you get acquainted with the basic functions without having to deal with changing too many parameters at once. It will help you build the basic files needed to run CDMetaPOP and give you some tips along the way.

The basic files you need to run CDMetaPOP are:

1.  **classVars.csv**: defines parameters related to the individuals according to their ages.

2.  **patchVars.csv:** defines the parameters related to patches (sites).

3.  **popVars.csv:** defines several parameters that relate to the population as a whole.

4.  **runVars.csv:** defines the basic parameters of your simulation (for example how many Monte Carlo runs). These parameters apply to all species.

Next, let's start with a **suggestion** on how to arrange your files, an example of how you could arrange your data directory structure:

```         
data/
├── runVars.csv
├── popvars/
│   └── popVars.csv
├── patchvars/
│   └── patchVars.csv
├── classvars/
│   └── classVars.csv
├── genes/
│   └── allelefrequencies.csv
├── cdmats/
│   ├── cdmat1.csv
│   ├── cdmat2.csv
│   └── cdmat3.csv
└── otherfiles/
    ├── correlation_matrix1.csv
    └── correlation_matrix2.csv
```


Click on the links below to start the shinyApps that will help you define the parameters for your first run and make sure to save them in a data path following the suggestion above.

**Remember to ALWAYS download your work from the PREVIEW TAB before closing the shinyApp**

![](images/DownloadPopvars.png){width="50%"}

-   **Start building a new classVars.csv**\
    [Open App](https://computationalecologylab.shinyapps.io/classVars/){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

<!-- -->

-   **Start building a new patchVars.csv**\
    [Open App](https://computationalecologylab.shinyapps.io/patchVars/){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

<!-- -->

-   **Start building a new popVars.csv**\
    [Open App](https://computationalecologylab.shinyapps.io/popVars/){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

<!-- -->

-   **Start building a new runVars.csv**\
    [Open App](https://computationalecologylab.shinyapps.io/runVars/){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

Although it may be counterintuitive, it is suggested to start preparing your files in the order given, as the runVars.csv requires to define a popVars.csv, which in turn designates both patchVars.csv and classVars.csv files.

Additional files can include distance matrices to guide individual movement, a correlation matrix to guide certain parameters at the patch level, and genetic files to initialize population genetic structure. But once you have the basic input files mentioned above you can start your basic simulations and get acquainted with the potential insights you can gain about your species in both space and time with CDMetaPOP.

If the input files are ready, proceed to the next step:

[➡️ Go to Run CDMetaPOP](run_cdmetapop.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}

For more detailed instructions you can refer to CDMetaPOP manual and several publications:

1.  [Manual](files/cdmetapop2_usermanual.docx).

2.  bla

3.  blabla

4.  blablabla
